title: tormentilla erecta f. strictissima
intro: Tormentilla erecta f. strictissima, also known as Leopold's tormentil, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the gentian family and is native to Europe, particularly in the mountainous regions of the Alps. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its roots, which contain iridoid glycosides, responsible for its therapeutic effects. Tormentilla erecta f. strictissima is used to treat various health conditions, including digestive issues, inflammation, and bacterial infections, thanks to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties. In herbal preparations, the plant's roots are often dried and used in teas, infusions, or tinctures to harness its medicinal benefits, while it is also used in some traditional folk remedies and homeopathic preparations.
